data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Adobe likely uses a microservices architecture to manage its diverse range of products and services efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Deployed across a distributed network with Kubernetes for orchestration.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: To enhance content delivery speed and reliability, Adobe utilizes a CDN for distributing content globally.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Akamai or a similar CDN solution to cache and serve content closer to user locations.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Adobe's infrastructure is heavily based on cloud services to enable scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Primary services are hosted on AWS and Azure, leveraging cloud computing and storage.

data.title: API-Driven Ecosystem
data.description: Adobe offers APIs for seamless integration across its applications and with third-party services.
data.technical_spec: RESTful APIs with OAuth 2.0 for authentication and JSON for data interchange.

data.title: Load Balancing and Auto-Scaling
data.description: To handle high traffic and ensure optimal performance, Adobe employs load balancing and auto-scaling mechanisms.
data.technical_spec: Load balancers via AWS Elastic Load Balancing, auto-scaling with cloud-native solutions.
